reasoning: The `is_topology` definition captures the standard topology axioms (contains univ, closed under binary intersection, and closed under arbitrary unions — with the empty union giving ∅). The Lean theorem takes a family `T : I → Set (Set X)` with each `T i` a topology, matching "family of topologies on X". The conclusion `∃! T'` with three conjuncts — T' is a topology, T' contains each T_i (`T i ⊆ T'`), and T' is contained in any topology T'' that contains all T_i — exactly captures "there is a unique smallest topology containing all the collections". The quantifier structure (∃! plus the minimality clause) faithfully encodes "unique smallest", without adding or removing hypotheses or changing the ambient types.
